name: Día de los Muertos (Mixquic)
traditions: La Alumbrada (thousands of candles in cemetery); silent vigil through the night; traditional food offerings; music by candlelight.
historicalBackground: Mixquic's celebration is among Mexico's most traditional, preserving Nahua customs in the ancient cemetery. The village was an island in Lake Chalco in Aztec times.
description: One of Mexico's most atmospheric Day of the Dead celebrations, where the ancient cemetery of Mixquic glows with thousands of candles.
